content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

December 24, 1990

Donald A. Barrier  
567 East Lassen Avenue, Space 218  
Chico, CA 95926

Re: Your Request for Advice  
Our File No. G-90-710

Dear Mr. Barrier:

Thank you for your November 15, 1990, letter discussing your concerns over campaign tactics used during the November election for the Chico City Council. I apologize for not responding sooner.

The jurisdiction of the Fair Political Practices Commission is limited to the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). Except for provisions not applicable to the Chico election, the Act does not address misleading campaign tactics, the issue raised in your letter. (See the enclosed advice letters, A-83-143 and A-84-106.) Nor does the Act permit the Commission to give advice concerning past conduct (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A),(c)(4)(A), copy enclosed) or to advise persons regarding others' duties under the Act. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(B),(c)(4)(C), copy enclosed). Because your letter is based on past conduct, and because you are not inquiring about your duties under the Act, we cannot give you specific advice.

By way of general advice, however, we can mention that the Act does contain a number of campaign disclosure requirements (Sections 84100-84514), as does the Code of Regulations (Regulations 18401-18439). The Commission has prepared a brief manual concerning campaign disclosure requirements pertaining to slate mail organizations, the type of organization referred to in your letter. (Enclosed is a copy of the manual.)

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

I hope this material is of some help to you. If you need additional information, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Steven R. Rhoads
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:SR:aa
Enclosures
